_schema.purpose: Per-service lease registry preventing duplicate Track-B signups across the mm-agent swarm. Workers MUST read this file (via git pull at cycle start) before signing up for a new DePIN/airdrop service. If a service is listed with status 'piloting' or 'won', do NOT duplicate — pick a different candidate. Lease TTL is 7 days for 'piloting' (matches Track-B 7-day kill gate); 'won' has no TTL until the queen demotes it.

claim_record.node: node-NN — node that owns the lease
claim_record.ts_claimed: ISO8601 UTC when lease was acquired
claim_record.ts_expires: ISO8601 UTC; piloting leases auto-expire 7d after ts_claimed
claim_record.ref_used: true | false — whether docs/REFERRALS.md ref was applied
claim_record.evidence: <=280 chars: signup result, first-cycle earnings, blockers

worker_protocol: 1. At cycle start: git pull master in $SKILLS_DIR.
2. Read services_claimed.json. Build set of names where status in {piloting, won} AND ts_expires > now.
3. When evaluating a Track-B candidate: if name in claimed-set AND owner != self, SKIP and log {"action":"signup_dedup_skip","service":<name>,"owner":<node>}.

4. Before signup: write claim to $SKILLS_DIR/config/hermes/swarm-skills/services_claimed.json (status=piloting), commit '[node-NN] claim <service>', push. If push fails (race), git pull --rebase; if claim now exists under another node, abort signup.
5. After 7d: if no earnings, set status=abandoned in next push. If earnings>$0, set status=won.
6. Queen pass periodically prunes status=abandoned older than 30d.
